cluster_label: Pedalling, jumping, balancing and ball play
gift_note: Good gift if the family has floor space for active indoor play — try cushions and tape first.
ownership_note: Many nurseries own Gonge or generic stone sets — borrow before duplicating.
educational_objective: Planned rule-based movement — step, pause, turn, jump. Not daredevil play. Try cushions or tape first; buy if a sturdier indoor path would help.
age_stage_nuance: At nearly three, movement shifts from random bouncing to following simple rules: step on green, pause, turn, jump to the next stone. Versus 31–33m hoops and beanbags (aim-and-throw), this band is about sequenced balance paths and indoor obstacle courses.
what_to_look_for: Reconfigurable paths; non-slip bases for hard floors; two sizes or heights for progressive challenge; sturdy enough for a parent to demo; stackable storage; age mark 2+ or 18m+ overlapping 34–36m; rule-game potential.
what_to_avoid: Single wobble boards; baby walkers; daredevil spring/bounce stones; identical duplicate sets; flimsy stones that slide on laminate; pure Ages 3+ only marks for this band; buying before trying cushions and tape.
methodology: Benchmarked UK balance-path lines (Gonge, Edx education specialists, budget 18m+ sets) against the planned step–pause–turn brief, explicitly rejecting pure 3+ marks that fail FF when band min is 34 months. Captured structured age_signals from live Liberty House, Dyslexia Shop, Shopedx and Amazon listings. Ranked by path-fit, age overlap, floor-grip/storage, distinct parent situations, and review honesty. URL-smoke and availability-screened every Top 5 primary on 2026-07-22 before locking buying_factor_memo and rank order.
buying_factor_memo: Five factors set order for nearly-three path play. First, rule-based path fit: reconfigurable stones or beam sections that support step–pause–turn games beat single wobble boards or daredevil bounce kit. Second, age overlap with 34–36m: only listings with Ages 2+/18m+/2–99 overlap — pure 3+ marks fail FF because band min is 34 months. Third, floor grip and storage: non-slip bases and stackable sets matter on UK laminate in flats. Fourth, distinct parent situations: premium progressive paths, budget starter, compact nesting, height sequencing, and low beam walking — not five similar stone packs. Fifth, evidence honesty: Gonge uses Amazon UK review depth on the same SKU; Edx and Tippi use specialist exemption where UK widgets are thin or bot-blocked rather than inventing counts.

preloved_sources_checked: Vinted/eBay UK listing patterns for Gonge River Stones

top_picks.age_mark_on_listing: Ages 2-99 years (Liberty House Toys product page)

key_specs.materials: PP plastic with TPE rubber rim
key_specs.other: Three steepness sides per triangle; stacks; 100 kg load

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Six stackable triangular stepping stones in two sizes. Each stone has three slope sides so you can dial difficulty when laying a path.
top_picks.ember_verdict: When cushions-and-tape has run its course, this is the proper path kit. Lay step–pause–turn rules on laminate without daredevil height. Each stone’s three sides progress difficulty without buying more. Pricey — borrow from nursery or try pre-loved before committing.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to follow simple movement rules like step, pause and turn rather than random crashing around
top_picks.why_it_fits: Variable steepness and two sizes create sequenced, rule-based paths — the core 34–36m shift versus earlier throw-and-aim games.
top_picks.caveats: Premium price; one set may feel short for long corridors — extend with tape markers rather than duplicating the same set.
top_picks.gift_note: Strong gift if the family has floor space and already plays active indoor games. Pair with a simple rule card.
top_picks.ownership_note: Many nurseries own these — ask before duplicating. Common on pre-loved sites.
top_picks.safety_notes: Non-slip rubber base. Supervise on hard floors until your child knows their limits — planned movement, not height daredevil play.
top_picks.rating_source: Amazon UK aggregated listing for Gonge 1137385 (same SKU)
top_picks.review_quality_note: Sustained praise for reconfigurability and floor grip; Liberty House shows verified UK owner reviews on check date.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Gonge River Stones regularly appear on Vinted and eBay UK; check rubber rim wear.
top_picks.substitute_if_unavailable: Tippi Sensory Stepping Stones 5-pack (budget path starter)
top_picks.rank_rationale: #1 over #2: three steepness settings and two stone sizes create richer rule-based paths than flat budget stones, which fits the nearly-three sequenced-movement brief better.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Primary Liberty House URL smoke-checked HTTP 200 on 2026-07-22. Age from live listing Ages 2–99. Ratings from Amazon UK same-SKU aggregate because Liberty House public count is thin (3 verified reviews).

top_picks.age_mark_on_listing: Balance toys for children age 18 months + (Amazon UK listing title/spec)

key_specs.materials: Plastic with textured top and rubber base

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Five stackable stepping stones with textured tops and rubber bases. Low profile for indoor lava-game paths on a budget.
top_picks.ember_verdict: If tape lines work but you want proper stones without Gonge money, this is the sensible starter. Textured tops give sensory feedback for cautious steppers. Lower and flatter than premium kit — fine for step-and-turn rules, less for big jumps. Replaces National Geographic here because pure 3+ marks fail FF for 34-month-olds in this band.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to enjoy short indoor obstacle courses with simple colour or texture rules before taller stones
top_picks.why_it_fits: 18 months+ mark overlaps 34–36m; five pieces enough for a hallway path with pause points at nearly three.
top_picks.caveats: Amazon UK review widget bot-blocked to agents — founder should sanity-check recent ratings on click. Less height progression than Gonge.
top_picks.gift_note: Easy-wrap budget gift for families testing whether path play sticks.
top_picks.ownership_note: Generic stepping-stone shape — many families already own a similar set.
top_picks.safety_notes: Rubber bases for laminate; low fall height. Supervise first sessions.
top_picks.rating_source: Amazon UK product page bot-blocked to automated fetch on 2026-07-22
top_picks.review_quality_note: Specialist exemption — budget tier with verified 18m+ age mark; human should confirm live review count on product page.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Lightweight sets common second-hand; check rubber base grip.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #2 over #3: much lower spend than Edx stumps while still clearing the 34–36m age gate with 18m+, making it the honest budget path before premium or specialist kit.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Chosen over National Geographic 6-pack because NG live mark is Ages 3+ only, which FF rejects when band min is 34 months. Primary URL returns HTTP 200 to smoke check; review count not scraped honestly.

top_picks.age_mark_on_listing: From 18 months upwards (The Dyslexia Shop listing copy)

key_specs.materials: Polypropylene with ribbed top, rubber-edged base

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Six ribbed, nesting stump stepping stones in three sizes, with a carry bag. Textured tops give sensory feedback under bare feet.
top_picks.ember_verdict: When storage is tight but you want more than flat stones, these nest into one stack with a carry bag. Ribbed tops suit children who pause to feel the surface before stepping. Good for step-and-pause paths; less exciting for big jumps.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to pause and look at where feet go before stepping, enjoying textured surfaces
top_picks.why_it_fits: Compact nesting plus three size grades supports progressive path difficulty in small rooms without cluttering the flat.
top_picks.caveats: Premium price for plastic; Shopedx lists £105.60 — shop around before buying.
top_picks.gift_note: Better gift for families who value tidy storage and sensory texture over brand recognition.
top_picks.ownership_note: Common in nurseries — check before gifting duplicates.
top_picks.safety_notes: Rubber-edged bases; ribbed tops reduce slip. Supervise first sessions on hard floors.
top_picks.rating_source: No public review widget on Dyslexia Shop / Shopedx product pages
top_picks.review_quality_note: Specialist exemption — Edx balance range stocked across UK education specialists with EN71 context; international Edx line well reviewed but not inventing UK counts.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Occasional nursery sell-offs; check rubber edge condition.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #3 over #4: nesting carry-bag storage and ribbed sensory tops solve small-flat clutter better than three tall hilltops that eat floor space.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Age 18 months+ captured from live Dyslexia Shop copy on 2026-07-22. Specialist education channel listing; UK review widget absent — exemption declared with written reason per trust gates.

key_specs.materials: PP plastic with TPE rubber feet
key_specs.other: Anti-overturn design; 100 kg load; pairs with River Stones

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Three stackable hilltop platforms at different fixed heights. Non-slip feet; designed not to tip when stepped on carefully.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Pick when the shift is step-up, pause on the tall one, turn, step down — height sequencing rather than flat hopping. Combine with River Stones for a mixed path. Hands nearby until confident; this brief is planned movement, not bouncing thrills.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to practise stepping up and down with deliberate pauses rather than running at speed
top_picks.why_it_fits: Three fixed heights teach planned up-down movement — distinct from flat stones and from 31–33m throw games.
top_picks.caveats: Only three pieces — short path unless combined with cushions or River Stones. Liberty House public review count is thin.
top_picks.gift_note: Impressive for active families with cleared floor space. Best after flat stones.
top_picks.ownership_note: Pairs with River Stones — check whether family already owns Gonge before doubling up.
top_picks.safety_notes: Non-slip rubber feet; anti-overturn design. Supervise height steps until your child can pause without hands.
top_picks.rating_source: Liberty House Toys shows 1 verified review on check date
top_picks.review_quality_note: Specialist exemption — Gonge OT/clinic standard; paired-use evidence from River Stones owner reviews and brand spec.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Gonge kit holds resale value; inspect rubber feet.
top_picks.substitute_if_unavailable: Gonge River Stones using steepest triangle side only
top_picks.rank_rationale: #4 over #5: fixed height progression adds step-up/step-down sequencing that low beam sections cannot, while staying inside the not-daredevil brief.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Ages 2+ from live Liberty House listing parsed to min_months 24 on 2026-07-22. Review count below 15 on primary — specialist exemption with Gonge brand + paired River Stones parent evidence instead of inventing Amazon counts for a different listing.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Six low balance-beam sections that link into straight or curved walking paths. Ribbed tops; six centimetres high — walk the line, not hop.
top_picks.ember_verdict: This is the path half of the category. Lay a walking line down the hall: heel-toe, pause at the end, turn, come back. Lower than stones so it suits cautious nearly-threes who need rule-based walking before jumping.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to practise careful heel-toe walking and stopping on command rather than big jumps
top_picks.why_it_fits: Beam paths train planned step-and-pause movement without jump height — distinct product type from all stone picks.
top_picks.caveats: Expensive for six plastic beams; needs a cleared hallway run.
top_picks.gift_note: Niche gift — best when parents want active indoor play and have space for a floor path.
top_picks.ownership_note: Tape on floor may suffice — only buy if child has outgrown tape and wants a raised edge.
top_picks.safety_notes: Low 6.4 cm height limits fall risk. Ensure sections sit flat on hard floors.
top_picks.rating_source: Shopedx page has no public review widget; NAPPA award editorial for Step-a-Trail line
top_picks.review_quality_note: Specialist exemption — NAPPA-recognised Edx line stocked by UK education retailers; not inventing Amazon.com counts on check date.
top_picks.substitute_if_unavailable: DIY painter's tape path (hold off buying)
top_picks.rank_rationale: #5: lowest jump height and a different motor pattern (heel-toe beam walk) so it rounds out the Top 5 without duplicating stone hopping picks.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Age 18 months+ from live Shopedx EDX63083 listing on 2026-07-22. NAPPA editorial corroborates Edx Step-a-Trail line quality; UK retailer primary used for URL/rating honesty.

skips.safety_notes: Single curved surface — not a path.

guidance_notes.title: Try cushions and tape first
guidance_notes.body: Painter's tape lines and sofa cushions cost nothing and test whether your nearly-three will follow path rules before you spend £50+. If they ignore tape but love lava games, stones may earn their keep.

guidance_notes.title: Borrow before buying premium Gonge
guidance_notes.body: Many nurseries and childminders own Gonge River Stones. A week's borrow tells you whether your child wants paths daily or twice then done.

guidance_notes.title: Pre-loved Gonge is sensible
guidance_notes.body: River Stones and Hilltops appear regularly on Vinted and eBay UK. Rubber rim wear is the check — slippery bases defeat the purpose.

guidance_notes.title: Hold off if floor space is tight
guidance_notes.body: A useful path needs at least two metres of cleared floor. If the living room is always cluttered, hoops and beanbags (31–33m) or park trips may beat indoor stones.

qa_summary.notes: Pick 1 meets hard rating bar via Amazon same-SKU. Picks 2–5 use specialist exemption where UK widgets thin or 3+ replacements moved to longlist. All Top 5 age_signals overlap 34–36m.

ingestion_ready.founder_review_notes: Re-research clears prior quarantine fails (unparsed age_signals, NG 3+ in Top 5, Hilltops review count). National Geographic moved to longlist backup with explicit 3+ caveat.
